question: What year is it?
Without looking at your hands, how many fingers do you have? (No cheating. Don't look at your hands.)
Blind since shortly after birth, this singer and pianist became one of Motown's biggest stars, recording classics like "Superstition" and "Sir Duke".
This German composer began losing his hearing in early adulthood and composed much of their later work while deaf, including the Ninth Symphony.
This pop superstar has spoken openly about living with fibromyalgia and has incorporated disability imagery into their work, including the wheelchair-centered "Paparazzi" music video.

In Pokémon, Shiny Pokémon are rare color variants, like the red Gyarados shown on the left. But for people with this type of color blindness, its red scales appear brownish-gray, making it look much closer to the common blue version.
Nintendo Switch Pokémon games allow for "Single-Handed Mode." Name a situational limitation where someone without a permanent impairment benefits from this.
answers: A player trying to increase the difficulty of the game's combat
A commuter holding onto a subway rail with one hand while playing
A user trying to conserve their controller's battery life
Playing the game while wearing a virtual reality (VR) headset

question: Often used to skip through dialogue, this adjustable game setting also helps reduce cognitive load for players with dyslexia and other processing disorders.
This painter’s severe cataracts caused blurred yellow, brown, and red vision that influenced his “Water Lilies” series.
This famous Mexican painter used art to explore their experience with chronic pain and disability following a streetcar accident.

This painting is described by the following alternative text: Portrait of a woman with a subtle smile, wearing a Renaissance-style dress and sitting with her hands folded against a misty landscape background.
This Blackboard product gives content an accessibility score and step-by-step guidance to improve items like color contrast, headings, and alternative text.
In Blackboard, it is more efficient for screen reader users to navigate by these, rather than using the tab key.

"I have struggled with every other screen capturing program I have tried. Using [this Blackboard product] was the first time I could start and stop a recording without help. It was liberating for me … job well done on this one!" –Visually impaired faculty member who uses JAWs to teach fully online.
This invention brightens your smile and makes your hygienist happy!
Making it easier for people to stay hydrated, this product was originally designed with accessibility in mind.
After developing severe carpal tunnel syndrome at the University of Delaware, inhibiting his ability to use the computer effectively, Wayne Westerman helped create this widely used technology.
